symbols: vertical and horizontal intersecting marks — the cross reduced to its essential abstract form
dark masses surrounding lighter vertical — the darkness that covered the land during the crucifixion
white vertical line amid darkness — the body of Christ or the light persisting through death
description: An abstract monochrome composition in black, white, and grey tones. Dark gestural brushstrokes and vertical lines dominate the image, with a prominent lighter vertical element near the center that could suggest an upright form or cross. The surface is heavily worked with layered, rough textures. Horizontal and diagonal marks intersect the vertical forms, creating a sense of tension and fragmentation. The overall impression is of violent energy contained within a dark, compressed space.
theological_description: This abstract work titled 'Crucifixion' strips the central event of Christian faith to its raw essence — suffering, darkness, and the intersection of vertical and horizontal that is the cross. By removing all figurative detail, the artist confronts the viewer with the crucifixion as pure event rather than historical illustration. The Gospels record that 'darkness came over the whole land' (Mark 15:33) during the crucifixion — a darkness this monochrome palette embodies. The faint vertical light amid the darkness echoes John's theology: 'The light shines in the darkness, and the darkness has not overcome it' (John 1:5). Paul proclaimed that 'the message of the cross is foolishness to those who are perishing, but to us who are being saved it is the power of God' (1 Corinthians 1:18). The abstraction forces the viewer beyond the familiar and comfortable to encounter the scandal of the cross in its terrible, irreducible reality.

scripture_refs.reason: Darkness over the land during the crucifixion — the monochrome darkness pervading the image
The message of the cross as the power of God — the cross abstracted to its essential meaning
Light shining in darkness — the white vertical form persisting amid the dark field
